addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Now let me look at how sibling functions (ClearStatFreqs) are specified for patterns.

Now I understand the structure. `result` may alias `stats1` (the actual call is `AddWeighedStatFreqs(&stats, 1.0, &laststats, 0.5, &stats)`), but the per-index read-then-write at the same index keeps the computation correct. Let me write the spec.

content.content: The file /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: static void AddWeighedStatFreqs(const SymbolStats *stats1, double w1,
+/* The three SymbolStats objects are distinct, fully-allocated objects. */
+/* Each result litlen (except the end symbol at 256) is the weighted blend of
+   the two inputs, truncated to size_t. */
+/* Each result dist is the weighted blend of the two inputs, truncated. */
+/* The end symbol is forced to 1. */

content.text: 0/12 — verifies but kills nothing. This looks like the depth-bound vacuity pattern (288-iteration litlens loop with FP arithmetic likely exceeds the depth bound before postconditions are reached). Let me confirm with a false-ensures probe.

input.command:
cp zopfli.c /tmp/zopfli.bak && python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
f='/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c'
s=open(f).read()
s=s.replace("__CPROVER_ensures(result->litlens[256] == 1)",
            "__CPROVER_ensures(result->litlens[256] == 1)\n__CPROVER_ensures(result->dists[0] == 999999)")
open(f,'w').write(s)
print("patched")
EOF
run-cbmc --function AddWeighedStatFreqs --file zopfli.c -I /tmp/cbmc-inc 2>&1 | grep -i "verified successfully\|FAILED\|verification fail\|kill score" | head

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-addweighedstatfreqs-loop-depth-vacuity
description: AddWeighedStatFreqs in zopfli.c verifies but 0/12; the 288-iter litlens FP loop exceeds the depth bound before postconditions are reached
metadata:
  type: project
---

`AddWeighedStatFreqs` (zopfli.c) verifies non-vacuously-looking but kills 0/12.
Distinct from the is_fresh-count cases: vacuity is driven by the **288-iteration
first loop** (`for i < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL` with `(size_t)(stats1->litlens[i]*w1 +
stats2->litlens[i]*w2)` FP arithmetic). Execution is truncated mid-first-loop by
the depth bound, so neither the dists postcondition nor litlens[256] check is
ever reached. Proven: a false `__CPROVER_ensures(result->dists[0] == 999999)`
still "verified successfully".

Loop contracts don't help: per [[cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored]] invariants are
silently dropped, and adding `__CPROVER_loop_invariant`/`__CPROVER_assigns` here
made the function unparseable ("Function missing from file") — the
[[cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse]] failure mode. No non-vacuous middle
ground. Strong sound spec (3 is_fresh SymbolStats, per-index forall blend
postconditions excluding index 256, litlens[256]==1) left in place.

Note `result` aliases `stats1` in the real call
(`AddWeighedStatFreqs(&stats,1.0,&laststats,0.5,&stats)`); per-index read-then-
write at the same index keeps it correct, but is_fresh forces distinctness so
that case isn't covered.

- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliFindLongestMatch vacuity](cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity.md) — ~17 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/157; each __CPROVER_forall needs a unique quantifier var name
- [CBMC ZopfliResetHash vacuity](cbmc-resethash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/25; ~230K body loop iterations (2×65536 head + 3×32768 window clears) exceed depth bound so postconditions never reached; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliUpdateHash vacuity](cbmc-updatehash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/40; 8 is_fresh objects + up-to-65535-iter run-length loop exceed depth bound; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliStoreLitLenDist vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/31; 8 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200, yet dropping any array is_fresh crashes goto-instrument; no non-vacuous middle ground
- [CBMC ZopfliVerifyLenDist shifted-forall](cbmc-verifylendist-shifted-forall.md) — won't verify; CBMC can't instantiate a shifted-index __CPROVER_forall (data[pos-dist+k]==data[pos+k]) to discharge the body equality assert. - [CBMC ZopfliWarmupHash replacement vacuity](cbmc-warmuphash-replacement-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/8; only 2 is_fresh objects (not depth), but replacing UpdateHashValue's contract on both body calls makes the exit unreachable (false ensures still "Verifies", --cover confirms); strong sound spec left in place

content.text:
Done. `AddWeighedStatFreqs` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` is now specified and **verifies successfully**.

## Summary

I added a strong, sound contract:
- **Preconditions**: all three `SymbolStats` pointers (`stats1`, `stats2`, `result`) are `__CPROVER_is_fresh`.
- **Frame**: `__CPROVER_assigns(result->litlens, result->dists)`.
- **Postconditions**: per-index `forall` capturing the weighted blend `(size_t)(stats1->x[i]*w1 + stats2->x[i]*w2)` for all litlens (excluding the end symbol at 256) and all dists, plus `result->litlens[256] == 1`.

**Kill score: 0/12 (vacuous).** I confirmed this is genuine depth-bound vacuity — a deliberately false `result->dists[0] == 999999` still "verified successfully." The cause is specific to this function: the 288-iteration litlens loop with floating-point arithmetic exhausts CBMC's depth budget before any postcondition is reached. Unlike most prior cases this isn't driven by `is_fresh` count but by loop length.

I tried the one available lever — loop contracts to abstract the loop — but they made the function unparseable (the documented tree-sitter loop-contract misparse), and per prior findings invariants are silently dropped anyway. There's no non-vacuous middle ground, so I left the strong sound spec in place and recorded the finding to memory.
